priorities: Align investments with biodiversity and ecosystem protection
Use wealth to fund concrete environmental philanthropy (reforestation, youth education)
Apply pressure on global brands to clean and verify their supply chains
redLines: Investments in companies that treat nature as a free resource
Companies with opaque or shell‑company supply chains that enable deforestation (especially palm oil)
Generic, non‑thematic or green‑washed ESG products
Any exposure to firms that do not publicly commit to cutting deforestation links
preferredSectors: Companies leading measurable rainforest restoration and indigenous land protection
Consumer‑goods firms with verified, transparent supply chains
Impact‑oriented equity focused on biodiversity leadership
avoidedSectors: Palm‑oil production and related supply chains
Companies with known deforestation or supply‑chain loopholes
Traditional high‑carbon sectors lacking credible ESG commitments

investmentBehavior.timeHorizon: long‑term (10+ years), focused on legacy and multigenerational impact
investmentBehavior.liquidity: low immediate liquidity needs; prefers cash to be swept into foundation accounts or reinvested in the Defensive Global Strategy
investmentBehavior.mandate: Biodiversity & Supply‑Chain Transparency Sustainable Equity Mandate

lifeEvents.description: Initial onboarding – wealth defined as an extension of a philanthropic mission after a sustainable‑agriculture exit.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Set the overarching ESG‑aligned mandate focused on corporate sustainability leadership.

lifeEvents.description: Diverted Q2 dividend streams to the foundation’s reforestation project in Peru.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Established a practice of routing investment income directly to environmental projects.

lifeEvents.description: Capital call of USD 250,000 for a land‑acquisition project in the Atlantic Forest, funded by trimming low‑scoring ESG fixed income.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Demonstrated willingness to reallocate assets toward high‑impact biodiversity land deals.

lifeEvents.description: Field trip to the Amazon; reinforced preference for companies investing in measurable rainforest restoration and indigenous protection.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Strengthened bias toward firms with on‑the‑ground conservation actions.

lifeEvents.description: Created a sub‑account for the foundation’s youth educational program on ecological restoration.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Added a dedicated channel for impact‑focused capital deployment.

lifeEvents.description: Preparing an extended summer trip to conservation sites in South America; requested June dividend payouts be swept to the foundation.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Re‑affirmed low‑liquidity stance and continued routing of cash flows to philanthropic accounts.

personalProfile.interests: sustainable agriculture, rainforest restoration, biodiversity, youth environmental education
personalProfile.quirks: insists on direct dividend sweeps to the foundation; rejects generic, non‑thematic investment products
personalProfile.rapportTriggers: Atlantic Forest land acquisition, reforestation projects in Peru and Brazil, biodiversity conferences, supply‑chain transparency discussions

keyQuotes: "I want to actively penalize companies that treat nature as a free resource."
"We expect Sarah to bring the same alignment of values and environmental transparency to our portfolio that Thomas did. No standard, generic investment products."
"If a company takes a brave, absolute stance against palm oil deforestation and actively puts money into reforestation, that is exactly where my money should be."
"Companies that don't protect their supply chains from ecological collapse won't survive the next decade anyway."
"We want our wealth to act as an extension of our philanthropic mission."
